name: Bobby Jindal
subject: WSJ: Kasich Could Be Trump’s Best Hope
body:
Dear Friend,

I recently wrote an oped for the Wall Street Journal discussing President Trump’s path to reelection. Those who write-off his chances should be careful. Increasing extremism on the democrat side combined with the strong potential of a third party candidate is just the scenario President Trump needs heading into 2020.
I hope you will take time to [read the full article](http://wc4.net/t?r=4673&c=6157&l=203&ctl=1787E:C15079EF2606CA7CC5DFADF68C9D72DD696548644412C47B&).

Best,

Bobby
* * *
**Kasich Could Be Trump’s Best Hope**
_An independent candidacy could split voters who dislike the Democrats but can’t abide the president._

The Wall Street Journal
Bobby Jindal
"...But the base isn’t enough to win, and Mr. Trump so far shows little sign of picking up new supporters. Thus a challenger is not sufficient—Mr. Trump needs a candidate who siphons off not only Republican Never Trumpers, but moderates who might otherwise vote for the Democrat. Fortunately for him, the Democrats are making themselves increasingly unattractive to those moderates."

**[Read more...](http://wc4.net/t?r=4673&c=6157&l=203&ctl=1787E:C15079EF2606CA7CC5DFADF68C9D72DD696548644412C47B&)**

* * *

[Unsubscribe](http://wc4.net/u?id=C15079EF2606CA7CC5DFADF68C9D72DD696548644412C47B)
---

name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: Let me know
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Klobuchar for Minnesota
body:
friend,

As the senior U.S. Senator from Minnesota, it's my job to get things done for Minnesotans.

I make it a point to get to know my constituents and their concerns. That's why I love meeting with people from every corner of the state. And I've found that the best way to learn what issues are the most important to Minnesotans is simply to ask.

So I'm asking: **At this challenging time, what do you think should be our top focus in the Senate?**
| [**Creating good-paying jobs**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=gsUbTzVKbFC056MRJzvMpzQ4R5YIzjWe)
---
| [**Investigating Russia 's interference in the 2016 election**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=ZgD3W3RvL7YZxaaeq0QmAjQ4R5YIzjWe)
---
| [**Protecting Social Security and Medicare**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=KgR2HRyvEM4Lqu5gZ2VbQNt%2BvDkVkKVc)
---
| [**Standing up for Americans ' healthcare, including women's health**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=2QMYcAiT%2F3dne5HmGjpXpDQ4R5YIzjWe)
---
| [**Tackling climate change and threats to our environment**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Mh2T%2BOAeNwWVnUcaeuljRzQ4R5YIzjWe)
---
| [**Something else**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=zzYjrifRIQ%2B96a38E80hIjQ4R5YIzjWe)
---

I appreciate your input!

Amy

name: Claire McCaskill
subject: what's on your mind?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McCaskill for Missouri
body:
Friend - some of the nonsense we're seeing in Washington right now is mind-boggling. Far-right Republicans in Congress are doing everything they can to pass President Trump's most extreme policies. Now more than ever, we need straight-talking Democrats who are willing to speak out for what's right.
That's what I'm trying to do every single day - cut through the nonsense and come up with some commonsense solutions to the problems we're all talking about around our kitchen tables.
But I want to make sure I'm focused on the issues that matter most to you. Can you take a quick survey to share what's on your mind right now?
[Share your thoughts](https://surveys.signforgood.com/mccaskill_welcome_survey?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=welcome1)
---
Thanks so much for being part of this. We've got a lot of fights ahead of us, but I know this team is tough as nails - and we're ready to get to work.
Claire

name: The White House
subject: ‘Congratulations to our new CIA Director’
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c5b98282f05633c6dc7090208da2e49e5fecc447bbb3a713db9b8fbf52d85f38ddb0c73eeeedb0f121b1cb59af1051b8093feb0b3ec476c3) The White House • May 18, 2018
## The Day Ahead
| • | President Donald J. Trump will address a summit on prison reform at the White House this morning. [Watch his remarks live at 11:30 a.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c5b98282f05633c65789809dc157df1d82cf369edd92555935a958d13b153af8f7ac59db90665db2822891e2406cac43c847ec98bc4fdbb4)
---|---
• | The President will meet with Secretary-General of the United Nations Antonio Guterres in the Oval Office this afternoon.
## ‘Congratulations to our new CIA Director’
**The Senate confirmed President Trump’s pick to lead the Central Intelligence Agency, Gina Haspel** , in a bipartisan vote yesterday. Director Haspel drew extensive support from the CIA workforce, as well as former intelligence leaders from both Republican and Democratic administrations. She replaces Mike Pompeo, who was confirmed as America’s 70th Secretary of State last month.
**“The safety and security of the American people** depend on capable intelligence leadership. Gina Haspel is the right woman at the right time," Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) said. President Trump [tweeted his congratulations](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c5b98282f05633c6b121000afdeac5a76845d52a4543f2784bc73161718807ef978412128d722ddcb7245bb139eee02b387508bc8924f29d) shortly after the Senate’s vote.
**Director Haspel’s biography made her an easy choice**. A 33-year veteran of the CIA, she’s earned multiple high-level honors, including the George H. W. Bush Award for Excellence in Counterterrorism. Years later, with the fight against al Qaeda heating up, she requested a transfer to the CIA’s Counter Terrorism Center. Her first day on the job was September 11, 2001.
[Meet Gina Hapsel, the new face of America’s intelligence community. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c5b98282f05633c6365a651c2c63dea6910a5795fac57f2099453e2e37f0bd769c001e3bc13ec2e5c0235e560fd47295179d43f445a27eab)

---
|

## Think again: ‘Sanctuary’ laws aren’t pro-immigrant
**America’s immigration challenge doesn’t end at our border**. Because of “sanctuary city” jurisdictions—many recently emboldened by new state laws in California—law enforcement officers face dangerous obstacles to protecting our communities from the effects of a broken immigration system.
**Under the guise of being pro-immigrant** , these jurisdictions privilege a small group of often-violent criminals at the expense of the safety and well-being of American citizens, law enforcement, and law-abiding immigrants alike. In effect, these jurisdictions assert a special exemption from Federal law. The consequences of their decisions, of course, do not remain in their own backyards.
**“They’re releasing these criminals, not by their houses**. . . . They’re releasing them by our houses,” San Jacinto Mayor Crystal Ruiz said at a White House forum with community leaders this week. “Every day we’re getting more and more reports from the police department about how they can’t arrest these people.”
[California’s ‘sanctuaries’ aren’t pro-immigrant—and local leaders are pushing back.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c5b98282f05633c66b1efa1505e1e0979ba1feac8d85a319cad5b8ed243ffb5517843767cefe5e005802250886ec173f2d7b16863df8ea0b)
**More** : [What you need to know about the stand against sanctuary state laws ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c5b98282f05633c6e1f1add5a5fcb2fe493c0d8688445f0d6acc33eabc1d45ac79ce01fe84762bd6607d1e7c121b77606e23e69b0a554db8)

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
**President Donald J. Trump with U.S. Air Force SSGT Charles Edwards of Mary Esther, FL, and CAPT Mark Kobelja, Director of WRNMMC at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center** | May 16, 2018

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=dcba6766c314ba4e05cea8bd3b1448cc5b30a71440b44cc2c4bfc48dee85810110e03f7a401fccaf0f4e605c3a987ebf015903a2c8baa322) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=dcba6766c314ba4ea6ffc4910ebae0c579e1c53a6427cd5a81a3dbcb54670f62363f5542b172a95887df1e4333c9ad81e348313175451e15) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4a20db5e556027792261fc434ef110b871cdb2fc477e4c158c0333b82f47571d6d3a90116c3c67a821d4ff41eb2591389b9c60fbfca08998d)
---

name: Survey (via NRCC)
subject: Election Day Survey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:

name: Amanda Brown Lierman
subject: We're doing things differently this year, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
The DNC is no longer in the business of mapping out our strategy once every four years. We are organizing year-round, up and down the ballot, in all 50 states for one simple reason: it's the only way
---
Our party is doing things differently this year, Derek.

The DNC is no longer in the business of mapping out our strategy once every four years. We are organizing year-round, up and down the ballot, in all 50 states for one simple reason: it's the only way to win elections.

You don't need me to tell you that the stakes this year are incredibly high -- but if we invest right now in grassroots organizing, we will be unstoppable in November. The only way to truly stop the madness of the Trump administration is to elect a Democratic Congress, and the only way to elect a Democratic Congress is to support Democratic candidates nationwide, starting right now.

**Make a $3 contribution to help Democrats organize and win.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e47294f/3638941261/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/abl-new-dnc?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e47294c/3638941261/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/abl-new-dnc?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e47294d/3638941261/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/abl-new-dnc?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e47294a/3638941261/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/abl-new-dnc?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e47294b/3638941261/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/abl-new-dnc?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e472948/3638941261/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/abl-new-dnc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")
** This election cycle, the DNC has launched four programs that will help transform the way our party engages with voters and communities across the country. I'll share a bit more about them below, but you should keep one thing in mind before you keep reading: **these programs will only succeed if millions of Democrats come together to support them.**

**Every ZIP Code Counts** is our party's commitment to organizing and competing everywhere to win elections in 2018, 2019 and beyond. The DNC is investing $10,000 in every state party in the country every month to help with this effort. By working at the local level, we will strengthen our state parties and ensure they have the resources to win elections.

**Winning with Partners in 2018** is a program launched by Deputy Chair Keith Ellison knowing that the DNC is one organization that is part of a broader movement committed to electing Democrats. Through a series of town hall meetings across the country, Keith is pulling together state party leaders, community activists, and organizations new and old. By listening to people on the ground, we can discover common goals and better mobilize our communities this year and in the years ahead.

**The IWillVote campaign** is an ambitious plan to reach 50 million voters by November. When more people vote, Democrats win! The four components of this program will be: 1) getting our community to commit to vote, 2) ensuring they are registered to vote, 3) providing voter education and protection, and 4) running a get-out-the-vote campaign.

**The Best Practices Institute** is an innovation and training hub to support candidates and state parties. We are investing in creative solutions while focusing on the proven best practices that will help develop a permanent infrastructure to continue our progress in 2019, 2020, and beyond.

Here's the thing, Derek. This is a road map for the DNC's strategy this year -- but our success won't come solely from these programs. It will come from people like you who speak up and seize your opportunity to have a say in which direction our country will take.

**[Contribute $3 right now if you're ready to elect Democrats nationwide who will get to work fighting for the values we share.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e472949/3638941261/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/abl-new-dnc?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+Contribute+3+right+now+if+youre+ready+to&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd")**

Thank you for your continued support of the Democratic Party. As long as we keep working together, I am confident that we'll help Democrats win up and down the ballot while fighting for the progressive values that are the core of our party.

All my best,

Amanda

Amanda Brown Lierman
Political and Organizing Director
Democratic National Committee

Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible. Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www.Democrats.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't your preferred email address, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e472956/3638941261/VEsO/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e472957/3638941261/VEsP/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e472954/3638941261/VEsHBQ/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fa9/4067b0a/59e5899fe/7e472955/3638941261/VEsHBA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180518_abl_nd&source=em_20180518_abl_nd&refcode=em_20180518_abl_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

body:
WASHINGTON TIMES:
**"Republicans and the White House view the Senate contest between Mr. Barletta and Mr. Casey as one of their best opportunities to flip a seat."**
Rush $5 now to protect Bob Casey:
[DONATE](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e67d3/51dbcc2a/b8d18/3a2ba32/2916597834/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDUxOF9mcjFfbmRfczIiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180518_fr1_nd_s2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

You can't miss this, it's too important.
Ever since Congressman Lou Barletta became the Republican nominee for US Senate on Tuesday night, this race has been taken up a notch. Barletta and his allies are coming after Bob hard, and this is only the beginning.

**_[We need to be prepared for the campaign to heat up _fast_ \-- give $5 or more today to help defend Bob Casey.](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e67d3/51dbcc2a/b8d18/3a2ba32/2916597834/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDUxOF9mcjFfbmRfczIiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180518_fr1_nd_s2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

President Trump has gotten personally involved in this race, and that isn't stopping anytime soon. He and Barletta are "good friends" \-- Trump personally recruited him to run for this seat and even recorded a robocall to help him get out the vote this week. On Wednesday, the president tweeted _three times_ about his buddy Barletta and even attacked Bob Casey by name.

Trump and his allies are desperate to keep their Senate majority, and they know that unseating Bob -- who has been a firewall against their disastrous agenda and who is from a state they won in 2016 -- would go a long way toward that goal.

**The president and his Republican allies have set their sights on flipping Pennsylvania. We need to make sure that doesn 't happen -- can you give $5 or more today to keep Pennsylvania's Senate seat blue?**

name: Team Lipinski
subject: unifying against hate

body:

Hi Peter,

As many of you know, our election in November is unique. While Democrats all across the country are mounting intense challenges against entrenched right-wing Republicans, our team has a different fight on its hands.

Our opponent in November is Art Jones, a Jew-hating, holocaust-denying white supremacist, who is quickly becoming one of the most sought-after spokesmen at white nationalist rallies.

This fight spans across party lines. We're trying to reach as many voters in IL-3 as possible to educate them about Jones and his history. As the sole Republican on the ballot, this is a serious problem.

[**Will you help us raise the resources we need to spread the word in our district? We 've got until Friday before we hit our next fundraising deadline. We need your help! **](https://act.myngp.com/el/iDLBO_dp1e_zWL_n_NU_3ldZMRPI8zFFJOIImXWjhIo=/P9Y6K04Fv-jx0F7UZj3zG6d4cK4aJ3QPjWJ1Rn4ov-s=?refcode=DL_EM_FR_WL_180518_1_3&amount=5&recurring=true&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=50023&city=Ankeny&state=IA&phone=&occupation=&employer=)

This week, another important voice weighed in on this campaign: our former opponent Marie Newman. Take a look:

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/iDLBO_dp1e_zWL_n_NU_3ldZMRPI8zFFJOIImXWjhIo=/P9Y6K04Fv-jx0F7UZj3zG6d4cK4aJ3QPjWJ1Rn4ov-s=?refcode=DL_EM_FR_WL_180518_1_3&amount=5&recurring=true&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=50023&city=Ankeny&state=IA&phone=&occupation=&employer=)
We are happy to have a unified front in such an important campaign as this. While Trump is snatching headlines left and right, we cannot let Jones slip under the radar and hide his anti-semitism from voters.

[**Help us educate this district and bring the fight to Jones. We need your help to fund our efforts. Please chip in here by Friday.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/iDLBO_dp1e_zWL_n_NU_3ldZMRPI8zFFJOIImXWjhIo=/P9Y6K04Fv-jx0F7UZj3zG6d4cK4aJ3QPjWJ1Rn4ov-s=?refcode=DL_EM_FR_WL_180518_1_3&amount=5&recurring=true&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=50023&city=Ankeny&state=IA&phone=&occupation=&employer=)

Thank you,

Team Lipinski

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: Do you follow me on Facebook?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
[](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add12/24747066/VEsH/ "http://www.rosenfornevada.com/")
[](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add12/24747066/VEsE/ "http://www.rosenfornevada.com/")
Friends,
Thanks for joining our team. I'm Jacky Rosen, and I'm running to serve as Nevada's new Democratic senator.
A little bit about me: after taking out loans and working two jobs to make ends meet, I was the first member of my family to graduate from college. I broke barriers and worked my way up the ranks as a woman in computer programming. I've lived in Nevada now for nearly 40 years - this is where I met my husband, raised my daughter and took care of my parents and in-laws. I gave back to my community as president of my synagogue in Henderson, and now I'm standing up to President Trump in Congress.
**What's motivated me my whole life has been the desire to see a problem, work together with others and find smart solutions.** We can find smart solutions in Washington, too, but only if we stand up to President Trump's hatred, bigotry, and narcissism. I'm running for Senate because I want to fight for the families I talk to every day who have been left behind.
**This Senate seat couldn't be more important -** already this year, Senator Heller has been a deciding vote to confirm Betsy DeVos, to allow states to defund Planned Parenthood clinics, and to let internet providers sell your data to the highest bidder without your consent. And for years, Senator Heller has been voting over and over again to dismantle the Affordable Care Act and slash Medicaid.
President Trump and Senator Heller are working together to rig the economy and the federal government for the super-wealthy at the expense of people who have to work for a living. Our voices aren't being represented.
Winning in Nevada is how we fix that. **This is going to be a huge Senate race for Democrats - our _best_ pickup opportunity this cycle - so I want to keep you updated on everything going on.**
Follow us on **[Facebook](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add13/24747066/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/rosenfornevada/")** and **[Twitter](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add10/24747066/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/RosenforNevada")** to get the most up-to-date news from the campaign.
| [](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add13/24747066/VEsD/ "https://www.facebook.com/rosenfornevada/")
---
| [](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add10/24747066/VEsA/ "https://twitter.com/RosenforNevada")
---
This is going to be a long and competitive race, and the only way to win it is together. Thanks so much for being part of our team.
Jacky
[Contribute](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add11/24747066/VEsB/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoianJfZnItZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IndlbGNvbWUxIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jr_fr-email?refcode=welcome1&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from the Rosen for Nevada email list, please click here.](https://secure.rosenfornevada.com/page/m/409dd48b/4fbb2af3/72f6056a/5e8add1e/24747066/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://jackyrosen.bsd.net/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
Contributions to Rosen for Nevada are not tax deductible.

name: Jason Yates
subject: Mike Huckabee’s call to Christians
body:
Peter,
Gov. Mike Huckabee recently summarized some of the critical problems we are facing today as a nation in an article he wrote for My Faith Votes.
If you haven’t read it yet, I encourage you to take a few minutes to read his short yet informative piece!
It is an important wake-up call for Christians today. We cannot fix what is wrong in our country if we do not first identify what is wrong.
Read Gov. Huckabee’s article today, _[Now Is the Time to Take A Stand, In Unity](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Farticles%2Fnow-is-the-time-to-stand-in-unity%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180518com01&n=1)._
We are honored to have Mike leading the charge to engage Christians across the nation to answer the call to stand for Biblical values in their communities.
**United. We Stand.**
Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Farticles%2Fnow-is-the-time-to-stand-in-unity%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180518com01&n=2)
©2018 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com).
To stop receiving emails, [click here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/unsubscribe?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180518com01&n=3).

subject: 🚨 Pence back in Indiana!! 🚨
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Peter, there's a lot going on, but we'll make it quick: |
---

| | **Mike Pence is in Indiana TODAY to raise money for Joe 's opponent
[PITCH IN TO FIGHT BACK >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c212c/7377f838/3f803f4b/51f96371/3107808301/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-penceback?refcode=20180518.1nd&amount=10")** Peter, there's a lot going on, but we'll make it quick:
1. **Who 's in Indiana today?** Vice President Pence. He's back AGAIN to host a fundraiser in Indianapolis for Rep. Braun that's supposed to bring in major cash from wealthy donors. A photo with the VP costs $10,000 -- and the organizers are already telling reporters they expect to raise at least $500,000 at the event!
2. **But who hasn 't been in Indiana?** Rep. Mike Braun. After he won the Republican primary last week, Rep. Braun _could have_ stayed in Indiana to meet with the Hoosiers from across the state for whom he claims he wants to work. But instead he flew to Washington to have lunch with Mitch McConnell -- who quickly embraced him as one of his own.

**Does this sound backwards, Peter? We agree. That 's why we're fighting our hearts out to send Joe back to the Senate to keep sticking up for Hoosiers, not special interests and wealthy donors. Will you pitch in to help us compete with Rep. Braun, McConnell, and Pence?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

name: Cory Booker
subject: We don't need another surprise
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
We need fighters like Tina in the Senate, people willing to fight for progressive values. | [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48a58/c1f7015/68b539ae/114378f9/1051299545/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/booker-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-18_booker&refcode=ema_2018-05-18_booker&utm_campaign=booker&utm_medium=ema") Friend, We've had some surprises in 2018. Red House seats flipped blue. Democrats fought competitive races in places that Trump won handily in 2016. And in Minnesota, we find ourselves needing to defend the Senate seat of one of our newest colleagues. Late last year, Tina Smith was appointed to the U.S. Senate, and let me tell you, she came to Washington ready to work. She didn't waste any time jumping in on issues that matter to her state and our nation. Tina is focusing on getting families better access to child care and expanding mental health services in schools. She's working on criminal justice reform, co-sponsoring my bill to "ban the box" for federal hiring so formerly incarcerated people have a fair shot at employment, and she's standing strong and voting NO when Trump nominates someone unqualified or unfit for their job. [**We need fighters like Tina in the Senate, people willing to fight for progressive values and not back down against the Trump administration. Will you help secure her seat in the Senate and contribute now?**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48a58/c1f7015/68b539ae/114378f9/1051299545/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/booker-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-18_booker&refcode=ema_2018-05-18_booker&utm_campaign=booker&utm_medium=ema") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [$10 »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48a58/c1f7015/68b539ae/114378fa/1051299545/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/booker-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-18_booker&refcode=ema_2018-05-18_booker&utm_campaign=booker&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=10")

---
| | [OTHER »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48a58/c1f7015/68b539ae/114378ff/1051299545/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/booker-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-18_booker&refcode=ema_2018-05-18_booker&utm_campaign=booker&utm_medium=ema&amount=25")
---
If we're going to hold the Trump administration accountable -- if we're going to hold Republicans accountable -- we need to win back the Senate majority, and our chance at the majority runs right through Minnesota.
With competitive races in the Senate, House, and Governor's office in Minnesota, you can bet the GOP is ready to spend big to turn Minnesota red. National Republican groups have already committed to spend millions. But we know when we get down to it -- the power of the people can beat the power of big money. **And now, Tina needs our help.**
[**That's why I'm asking you to support Tina's special election campaign and chip in $10 now. We need grassroots donors like you to help make sure we elect strong candidates like Tina to take our country forward -- not backwards.**](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc48a58/c1f7015/68b539ae/114378f9/1051299545/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/booker-smith?utm_source=ema_2018-05-18_booker&refcode=ema_2018-05-18_booker&utm_campaign=booker&utm_medium=ema")
Thank you for keeping up the fight,
Cory

With competitive races in the Senate, House, and Governor's office in Minnesota, you can bet the GOP is ready to spend big to turn Minnesota red. National Republican groups have already committed to spend millions. But we know when we get down to it -- the power of the people can beat the power of big money. And now, Tina needs our help. "" Thank you for keeping up the fight, Cory ""

subject: Just tell us where to mail it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
#
Hey there,
Wow! Since we first emailed you about the "Beto Backers" program, a huge number of supporters have stepped up to make monthly recurring donations to our campaign.
Our records say you haven't joined the Beto Backers yet, but don't worry! It's incredibly easy to set up your monthly recurring donation -- and if you sign up this week, we'll send you a Beto Backers button in the mail so you can show your support with pride.
**[We need you to add your monthly recurring contribution today. Sign up right now and we'll send you a special "Beto Backer" button in the mail. The average monthly donation is just $18.](https://secure.betofortexas.com/page/m/6eb92e9d/3b1d797/63d3e5b0/35a865c3/821514831/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bo-180516-recurring-donor?refcode=ema180518&amount=18&recurring=true")**

These buttons are a small way to thank you for having Beto's back in this tough campaign. We've got a long road ahead, and we're going to need you with us at every turn.
Your monthly recurring contribution will directly fund our campaign to elect Beto and take on Ted Cruz. Every donation helps us pay for things like clipboards and walk packets at our field offices across Texas, and keeping gas in the minivan that's helping Beto reach each of Texas' 254 counties.
**[Can you take the next step and become a"Beto Backer" today? The average monthly donation from our supporters is $18, but any amount you can afford will mean a lot to our team. Your contributions will sustain our campaign all the way to Election Day this November. ](https://secure.betofortexas.com/page/m/6eb92e9d/3b1d797/63d3e5b0/35a865c3/821514831/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bo-180516-recurring-donor?refcode=ema180518&amount=18&recurring=true")**
Thanks for all you're doing to support Beto. We can't do it without you.
Team Beto
**[CHIP IN $18 A MONTH](https://secure.betofortexas.com/page/m/6eb92e9d/3b1d797/63d3e5b0/35a865c3/821514831/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bo-180516-recurring-donor?refcode=ema180518&amount=18&recurring=true")**
\-------
_P.S. If you signed up to become a Beto Backer very recently and are receiving this email, don't worry! Our records are still updating. We will send you your button in the mail soon._

* * *
Paid for by Beto for Texas

subject: Big news!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ian Todd for Congress
body:
This is a big moment for our campaign.
Just yesterday we managed to schedule a live interview with The Young Turks on their show Rebel Head Quarters. We are incredibly thrilled about this opportunity and we have you to thank. Without your support, none of this would be possible. Tune in on July 3rd to find Ian talking to talk show host Cenk Uygur about our campaign in 2018.
**Our campaign is gaining momentum, and its time we share that news with the country. **
Between now and then, we will keep on knocking doors, making phone calls, and hosting events across the district. With your help, I believe we can win in 2018!
Together we can do big things.
Thanks for your support,
Benjamin Carollo

name: Jerusalem Demsas
subject: Let's make a date
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Join us for a canvassing day of action!

Friend --
Got plans on Saturday?
If you're looking for some, look no further: Join us for a rally to kick-off a Canvassing Day of Action with the Communications Workers of America and award-winning actor Harry J. Lennix.
**[It's all going down at 10:00 am in Beltsville on Saturday -- RSVP now to join in! ](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a611/7ab0e2c4/7fb16b48/349a4032/3640831112/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/183590908862011")**
Here are the details:
**What:** Canvassing Day of Action
**Where:** 10782 Rhode Island Avenue
Beltsville, MD 20705
**When:** Saturday, May 19
10:00 am
[RSVP NOW »](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a611/7ab0e2c4/7fb16b48/349a403d/3640831112/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/183590908862011/")
---
This is a great moment to get involved -- Ben's a community organizer who spends every moment he can meeting working folks, and loves opportunities like this to meet new volunteers and voters.
Which is great, because after the rally, our team will be canvassing across Maryland to build the movement of working families ready to get big things done again.
If you're a seasoned organizer, or a brand new volunteer, I promise you'll have a ton of fun and do a lot of good -- join us!
**[RSVP now to join Ben and actor Harry J. Lennix on Saturday in Beltsville!](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a611/7ab0e2c4/7fb16b48/349a403d/3640831112/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/events/183590908862011/")**
See you there,
Jerusalem
Jerusalem Demsas
Deputy Communications Director
Ben Jealous for Governor

[CHIP IN »](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a611/7ab0e2c4/7fb16b48/349a403c/3640831112/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bj-st5-18?refcode=OHQ05188A_footer")
---

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, [click here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a611/7ab0e2c4/7fb16b48/349a403f/3640831112/VEsD/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/update-your-information?source=emfooter"). name: Nina Ahmad
subject: Thank you & moving forward
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for PA
body:
Dear Friends,

Thank you. Thank you for your support, for your belief in me, and for your faith that we deserve new voices in our leadership.

The support I received from Philadelphia to Pittsburgh and everywhere in between- and from New York to California, from people who couldn't even vote in the PA Lt. Governor race!- absolutely blew me away. The energy I saw from women, immigrants, and people of color who too often don't see themselves reflected in positions of power was incredibly inspirational and a constant reminder of why I was running.

After just 11 busy weeks of campaigning, we won an unbelievable 182, 233 votes across the state. We won the most votes in 15 counties, including Montgomery, Bucks, Delaware, Lehigh, Northampton, Lancaster, Dauphin, Cumberland, Monroe, Lebanon, Adams, Franklin, Union, Snyder, and Montour. With your support, I have earned a strong 2nd place finish in spite of a poor ballot position, defeating the incumbent Lt. Gov. Mike Stack, a long-term Philadelphia elected official, and leading in Southeastern Pennsylvania by winning Philadelphia and Harrisburg media markets!

I'm astounded by and deeply grateful for the tremendous outpouring of support I have received over the past 11 weeks. Thank you for being part of one of the best experiences of my life so far.

Until next time,

Nina

P.S. In running the best operation we could and trying to reach as many voters as possible, we accrued debt over the course of the campaign. If you can pitch in any amount to help cover some of that debt, we'd be so grateful. Please consider giving any amount here:

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/hj9D4TUkXxOkSpA47q9Lp-qw4P6_-kTMticS3jcNHRc=/XQo51avq6KQYQ0ZKpouEd69ifYIZiCEFQ3x2GjnEUZA=)

Paid for by Nina for PA
---
Nina for PA

Philadelphia PA 19107 United States

name: Florida Democrats
subject: if Trump fires Robert Mueller
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Florida Democratic Party
body:
President Trump is ramping up his vicious attacks on Special Counsel Robert Mueller - and that means the Russia investigation is hitting home. |
---
| Listen, the Special Counsel and his team must be allowed to complete their investigation into Russia's election meddling. If Trump fires Robert Mueller, it will precipitate a constitutional crisis, permanently damaging the integrity of our democratic institutions. [**We still need 11,400 signatures to demand that Congress protect Mueller. Add yours here.**](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4be9e/1a5213b3/6ddcb246/718ace8f/1265870232/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIn0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/ProtectRobertMueller/?code=FLDems&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-defend-mueller-ja%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2%26amount%3D10%26recurring%3D1&firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=1+-+We+still+need+11400+signatures+to+demand&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&source=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2")

**_Begin original message:_** [**Demand Congress protect Robert Mueller from Trump!**](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4be9e/1a5213b3/6ddcb246/718ace8e/1265870232/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIn0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/ProtectRobertMueller/?code=FLDems&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-defend-mueller-ja%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2%26amount%3D10%26recurring%3D1&firstName=&lastName=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+Demand+Congress+protect+Robert+Mueller+f&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&source=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2") [SIGN YOUR NAME ->](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4be9e/1a5213b3/6ddcb246/718ace89/1265870232/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIn0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/ProtectRobertMueller/?code=FLDems&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-defend-mueller-ja%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2%26amount%3D10%26recurring%3D1&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=3+-+SIGN+YOUR+NAME+rarr&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&source=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2") Heads up: President Trump is ramping up his vicious attacks on Special Counsel Robert Mueller - and that means the Russia investigation is hitting home.

Trump is lashing out, describing the investigation as nothing more than a witch hunt. And with Rudy Giuliani leading the charge, his team is now trying to use the one-year anniversary of the Russia probe to pressure Mueller into publically exonerating Trump.

**Trump is willing to do anything to prevent Mueller from completing his investigation.

[In honor of the special investigation's one-year anniversary, we're demanding Congress take action IMMEDIATELY to defend Robert Mueller from White House interference. We need 20,000 signatures in the next 48 hours to send our message. Add your name here.](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4be9e/1a5213b3/6ddcb246/718ace88/1265870232/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIn0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/ProtectRobertMueller/?code=FLDems&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-defend-mueller-ja%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2%26amount%3D10%26recurring%3D1&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=4+-+In+honor+of+the+special+investigationrsq&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&source=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2")**

Even Vice President Mike Pence is weighing in, saying it's time to wrap up the investigation.

It's clear the Special Counsel's investigation into Russian meddling - and worse - in the 2016 election is reaching Trump's inner circle. Robert Mueller is not afraid to go after the truth, even if it leads to the Oval Office.

Cornered, the president will do anything to save his neck. But the American people deserve to know the truth about what the Russians did with our elections in 2016 - and how they did it.

[**Raise your voice now and demand that Congress protect Robert Mueller and his investigation from Donald Trump 's petulance. We've only got 48 hours - sign on here.**](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4be9e/1a5213b3/6ddcb246/718ace8b/1265870232/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZHYVhKemRFNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVnNZWE4wYm1GdFpTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01TVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZCUkVSU01pVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIn0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/ProtectRobertMueller/?code=FLDems&redirect=http%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Ffdp-defend-mueller-ja%3Frefcode%3DFDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2%26amount%3D10%26recurring%3D1&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=5+-+Raise+your+voice+now+and+demand+that+Con&utm_campaign=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&source=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2&refcode=FDP_EM_JA_180DO_180518_1_2")

Thanks for taking action.

Florida Dems

name: Jill Vogel
subject: Legislative Session Update May 2018
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Dear Peter,
With the wrap up of regular session in March, we had considered 3,700 bills and resolutions and reached agreement on all but the most important bill of session--the two-year budget. We adjourned sine die with no budget, a giant gap between the House and Senate proposed budgets and a disagreement over healthcare. The two most recent budget impasses lasted until June, but new budgets were adopted days before the old budgets expired and Virginia avoided a shutdown. To keep the process moving this time, the Senate passed a resolution calling for a special session to continue work. In the meantime we have worked through the spring, including meeting for the Veto Session and regular Finance meetings. Since so much time has passed, a quick recap of session before the full budget update is probably warranted. While most of the attention during the 60-day session focused on where leadership in the House and Senate were in disagreement over healthcare, far more of our time was spent working hard on issues where we agree. Many of us face difficult challenges in our Senate districts and my willingness to support my colleagues and their willingness to support me makes all of the difference in session being productive. In this session we saw headway on matters related to agriculture, transportation, energy, the environment, jobs and education. Bills that I patroned or co-patroned dealt with health insurance reform, I-81 improvements, CBD oil, health insurance coverage for autism, a ban on improper personal use of campaign funds, industrial hemp, post-election audits, redistricting reform, securing part of Camp 7 Property for Clarke County and addressing a tax issue for Middleburg. Another major priority for me was the hard fought bill that allows for historic horse racing in Virginia. I have long supported this as a way to restore Virginia's great history of racing and to re-open Colonial Downs. It is important to agriculture, as it pumps life and money back into our equine industry which has slowly seen investment bleed out to Maryland, Pennsylvania and Kentucky along with the loss of millions of dollars in jobs. This week I was in Richmond for our Senate Finance Committee meetings where we seemed to have reached an intractable position on the budget. The issue is healthcare. Currently, the House budget includes Medicaid expansion and the Senate budget does not. During this legislative session and in previous sessions, I have voted against Medicaid expansion. I have always held that it was exceptionally treacherous to expand if there was any chance that Congress would repeal and replace the program and leave Virginia on the hook for billions of dollars that we could not replace in our budget. In past years, the full Senate has likewise rejected expanding Medicaid because ongoing spending commitments threatened funding for other priorities, like roads, schools, and public safety. Currently, without expansion, Medicaid takes almost one-fourth of our general fund budget and it is growing at 8.9% annually. Opponents of expansion cite costs in other states that are 157% higher than originally projected. Our focus should be on addressing current costs and issues with the system. That said, if we move forward it should be our highest priority to engage conservatives in this discussion and push for reforms in Medicaid. A plan to expand Medicaid has passed in the House and certainly as we debate this in the Senate I am making every effort to ask for reforms, limits and constraints on the program that would make a big difference in the impact on the state and in some cases, put us in a better posture than we are with the current program. If we crack open Medicaid this session, then we certainly should take the opportunity to do the things that we know the system desperately needs and that reform groups have asked for around the country -- like limits on how much it can consume of the overall budget, monitoring systems to protect against fraud, accountability, a framework to help the most needy and real work requirements--none of which we have now in Virginia. Stay tuned. The Senate meets again next week and hopefully we will make headway on a final budget that we can vote on before our deadline. I appreciate your visits and feedback during the session. I hope that you will contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached at 540-662-4551 or by email at [district27@senate.virginia.gov](mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov). Our office appreciates the opportunity to serve and we are very grateful for your continued support.

---
|
---
| Senator Jill Vogel
---
| |
---
| Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
---
| Follow Jill on Facebook and Twitter for Campaign Updates

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | Dear Peter, With the wrap up of regular session in March, we had considered 3,700 bills and resolutions and reached agreement on all but the most important bill of session--the two-year budget. We adjourned sine die with no budget, a giant gap between the House and Senate proposed budgets and a disagreement over healthcare. The two most recent budget impasses lasted until June, but new budgets were adopted days before the old budgets expired and Virginia avoided a shutdown. To keep the process moving this time, the Senate passed a resolution calling for a special session to continue work. In the meantime we have worked through the spring, including meeting for the Veto Session and regular Finance meetings. Since so much time has passed, a quick recap of session before the full budget update is probably warranted. While most of the attention during the 60-day session focused on where leadership in the House and Senate were in disagreement over healthcare, far more of our time was spent working hard on issues where we agree. Many of us face difficult challenges in our Senate districts and my willingness to support my colleagues and their willingness to support me makes all of the difference in session being productive. In this session we saw headway on matters related to agriculture, transportation, energy, the environment, jobs and education. Bills that I patroned or co-patroned dealt with health insurance reform, I-81 improvements, CBD oil, health insurance coverage for autism, a ban on improper personal use of campaign funds, industrial hemp, post-election audits, redistricting reform, securing part of Camp 7 Property for Clarke County and addressing a tax issue for Middleburg. Another major priority for me was the hard fought bill that allows for historic horse racing in Virginia. I have long supported this as a way to restore Virginia's great history of racing and to re-open Colonial Downs. It is important to agriculture, as it pumps life and money back into our equine industry which has slowly seen investment bleed out to Maryland, Pennsylvania and Kentucky along with the loss of millions of dollars in jobs. This week I was in Richmond for our Senate Finance Committee meetings where we seemed to have reached an intractable position on the budget. The issue is healthcare. Currently, the House budget includes Medicaid expansion and the Senate budget does not. During this legislative session and in previous sessions, I have voted against Medicaid expansion. I have always held that it was exceptionally treacherous to expand if there was any chance that Congress would repeal and replace the program and leave Virginia on the hook for billions of dollars that we could not replace in our budget. In past years, the full Senate has likewise rejected expanding Medicaid because ongoing spending commitments threatened funding for other priorities, like roads, schools, and public safety. Currently, without expansion, Medicaid takes almost one-fourth of our general fund budget and it is growing at 8.9% annually. Opponents of expansion cite costs in other states that are 157% higher than originally projected. Our focus should be on addressing current costs and issues with the system. That said, if we move forward it should be our highest priority to engage conservatives in this discussion and push for reforms in Medicaid. A plan to expand Medicaid has passed in the House and certainly as we debate this in the Senate I am making every effort to ask for reforms, limits and constraints on the program that would make a big difference in the impact on the state and in some cases, put us in a better posture than we are with the current program. If we crack open Medicaid this session, then we certainly should take the opportunity to do the things that we know the system desperately needs and that reform groups have asked for around the country -- like limits on how much it can consume of the overall budget, monitoring systems to protect against fraud, accountability, a framework to help the most needy and real work requirements--none of which we have now in Virginia. Stay tuned. The Senate meets again next week and hopefully we will make headway on a final budget that we can vote on before our deadline. I appreciate your visits and feedback during the session. I hope that you will contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached at 540-662-4551 or by email at mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov. Our office appreciates the opportunity to serve and we are very grateful for your continued support. | | Senator Jill Vogel | | | Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186 | Follow Jill on Facebook and Twitter for Campaign Updates == == | | Vogel for Virginia | 45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100, Warrenton, VA 20186 | | Sent by mailto:info@senatorjillvogel.com in collaboration with |

name: Team Morse
subject: VOTING INFO: Monday is the last day to register to vote!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jessica Morse for Congress, a grassroots campaign
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/zhmsENXgvGdy5YdhLbziy0Rd6et0osT4l1iGpS2CYQ4=?recurring=auto&refcode=email_ngp_fr_20180518_TM_votingguide_in-district_logo)

Peter, this is it!

We're in the final stretch of this primary, and we'll be sprinting 24/7 until polls close on the evening of June 5th. Today, we wanted to drop you a note with all the information you'll need to make your voting plan and help your friends and family do the same.

_**CHECK YOUR VOTER REGISTRATION STATUS**_
The last day to register to vote in the June 5th primary is Monday, May 21st.
* [_**Check your voter status now**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/FBcm3_F4mq_b9vAvlkAT95DZF_drgaXJlYRsug4KKv8=)--make sure all your information is up to date!
* If you're not registered, or have friends or family who need to get registered, [_**you can register to vote online**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/yNTNgTk8wMF_i0x6cIJjvlk1ToLgm2WHURi3N-z8UyA=)--all you need is your California driver's license or ID card number, the last four digits of your SSN, and your birth date.

_**MAILING YOUR BALLOT**_
* Got five minutes? [_**Get that ballot in the mail!**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/_Y2dqHqu9xOo1gZ1bs2RcV5JFmbzie0u6tcJcjhwIio=) We want to get as many votes banked for Jessica as possible before Election Day.
* Didn't get a ballot, but interested in voting by mail? There's still time to [_**apply to vote by mail**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/_-n4WF_uwNrft7m6M3-FPJkJr6RVis3o-Zvew2iqniQ=)--but you'll want to get it in as soon as possible (your application must be sent by Friday, May 25th).

_**ELECTION DAY**_
Planning to vote on June 5th? [_**Find your polling place here.**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/QYn2QmhGg3NXvq-PKytVSPaFwpuxn3dOvbnzMkmNs-E=)

Now's the time to make your plan for how and where to vote for Jessica. **Are you voting before or after work? Going on your own or with a neighbor?** Once you've made your plan, make sure you help everyone around you vote, too.

Thanks,
Team Morse

P.S. Help us spread the word--forward this email to three friends or family members in our district who may need information about how to make their plan to vote.

**[CONTRIBUTE>>](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/zhmsENXgvGdy5YdhLbziy0Rd6et0osT4l1iGpS2CYQ4=?recurring=auto&refcode=email_ngp_fr_20180518_TM_votingguide_in-district_contribbutton)**
---
** **

Paid for by Jessica Morse for Congress, a grassroots campaign.
---
Jessica Morse for Congress
1079 Sunrise Ave
Ste B 275
Roseville CA 95611 United States
_[unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/7sasE-YLDlj_6eQnpVEi5kwsoGWe21Exsct5YAp1VX4=/IKzLRsGOVgxK2Ul73Xz6nroCX2ee20C_YsH85fDQym0=)_

subject: BREAKING: Prosecutor says nothing to see here
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
BREAKING: Prosecutor sees no reason to bring a case against Eric Greitens related to The Mission Continues.

Associated Press
12:21 PM, May 18, 2018

COLE COUNTY, Mo. -- A Missouri prosecutor says he won't be filing any charges against Gov. Eric Greitens related to a campaign finance report about his use of a charity donor list.

Cole County Prosecutor Mark Richardson issued a brief statement Friday confirming he received information from Attorney General Josh Hawley's investigation into Greitens' campaign.

Richardson said he decided not to file the criminal charge suggested by Hawley's office.

Read More (https://www.kshb.com/news/state/missouri/no-charge-on-greitens-on-campaign-finance-report?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=0c6e612765-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_03_21&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-0c6e612765-99411629)

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on Facebook (https://facebook.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=0c6e612765-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_03_21&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-0c6e612765-99411629) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=0c6e612765-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_03_21&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-0c6e612765-99411629) . Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. If they become too much, you can unsubscribe (https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=0c6e612765) .

name: =?utf-8?Q?Paul=20Spencer=202018?=
subject: Join Us at Our Election Night Watch Party!
body:
It’s been a hell of a ride, and now the day we’ve been working for is just around the corner: Primary Day is this Tuesday, May 22nd! | | | | [ ](https://paulspencer2018.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c286683455dc2d807b90e667d&id=0ae519919e&e=f1637b94bf)
---
| | | Friends,
It’s been a hell of a ride, and now the day we’ve been working for is just around the corner: Primary Day is this Tuesday, May 22nd! We’ve spent the last 10 months travelling to communities all across the Second District and met thousands of voters who are excited to vote for a candidate who will always put working families first and who rejects special interests and big donors.

In these last few days, we are making a big push to turn out every voter we can and defeat our establishment opponent and continue the fight to make the voices of working people heard in the Second District. And when we have knocked all the doors we can and called every possible voter, I want to invite you to our Election Night Watch Party! We will watch the results roll in at our campaign headquarters in North Little Rock, and it’s sure to be a great time as we say goodbye to the primary and look forward to the general election!
---
| [ELECTION NIGHT WATCH PARTY](https://paulspencer2018.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c286683455dc2d807b90e667d&id=1d2209dfdb&e=f1637b94bf "ELECTION NIGHT WATCH PARTY")
---
| And if you’re interested in volunteering your time on Election Day itself, please let us know by replying to this email or calling our office at _**_501-372-7285_**_. We will be all across the district waving signs, handing out information, and knocking on doors to get out the vote, and we appreciate any help you can give, even if it's just during your lunch break. I can’t wait to see the results of what we’ve done together, so let’s make sure we win every vote we can in this primary!
---
| [Express Donate: $10](https://paulspencer2018.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c286683455dc2d807b90e667d&id=832e2e5f5a&e=f1637b94bf "Express Donate: $10")

| Dear Peter,
I cannot stand idly by and watch as an ever-expanding government erodes the principles of liberty that make America exceptional. After generations of unprecedented corruption, I feel compelled to fight for the principles of limited government and personal responsibility -- the ideals for which our founding fathers fought and bled.
"_All that is required for evil to triumph is for good men to do nothing._ "
\- Edmund Burke
'We the People' are facing unprecedented corruption from self-serving politicians, unchecked bureaucrats, and a propagandist media. We must fight back or our children and grandchildren will never know the blessings of freedom which we have enjoyed. We must fight back because our nation faces national security threats the likes of which no Democrat should ever be entrusted. I will use my platform as a candidate and as a Senator to battle any forces which seek to control or limit our pursuit and our right to prosper.
**President Trump needs reinforcements** President Trump is engaged in a multi-front war against the political establishment in both parties, against the media, and against powerful globalism advocates. As a Senator, I will boldly stand with the President to "drain the swamp" and fight for the America First Agenda.

**Powered by the Grassroots**
It is my campaign's mission to work with you to amplify your voice in this upcoming midterm Senate election. This isn't a campaign about me, this is a campaign about ALL of us -- and it will take ALL of us to #FireClaire.
Let's do this together now and for tomorrow.

Sincerely,

---

clean_body: America First--It's going to take ALL of us. | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | Dear Peter, I cannot stand idly by and watch as an ever-expanding government erodes the principles of liberty that make America exceptional. After generations of unprecedented corruption, I feel compelled to fight for the principles of limited government and personal responsibility -- the ideals for which our founding fathers fought and bled. "All that is required for evil to triumph is for good men to do nothing. " \- Edmund Burke 'We the People' are facing unprecedented corruption from self-serving politicians, unchecked bureaucrats, and a propagandist media. We must fight back or our children and grandchildren will never know the blessings of freedom which we have enjoyed. We must fight back because our nation faces national security threats the likes of which no Democrat should ever be entrusted. I will use my platform as a candidate and as a Senator to battle any forces which seek to control or limit our pursuit and our right to prosper. President Trump needs reinforcements President Trump is engaged in a multi-front war against the political establishment in both parties, against the media, and against powerful globalism advocates. As a Senator, I will boldly stand with the President to "drain the swamp" and fight for the America First Agenda. Powered by the Grassroots It is my campaign's mission to work with you to amplify your voice in this upcoming midterm Senate election. This isn't a campaign about me, this is a campaign about ALL of us -- and it will take ALL of us to #FireClaire. Let's do this together now and for tomorrow. Sincerely, | | | | | | | | | | | | mailto:contact@sykesforsenate2018.com?subject=Helping%20Team%20Sykes | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | | PAID FOR BY COURTLAND SYKES FOR SENATE 2018 Courtland Sykes for Senate,

body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5b5f/24c69fd9/1bd029493/188ecf2d/2622747595/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEQ49N_digital_email_20180518_DomesticGag_inact&source=WQEFEQ49N_digital_email_20180518_DomesticGag_inact")
---

Peter,

Donald Trump is bringing the global gag rule home.

His administration is advancing a proposed rule that would cut off Title X funding (which is for family planning services) to clinics that provide abortion care or referrals for abortion care -- like Planned Parenthood. In short: **It's a domestic gag rule.**

And it would put patients' lives at risk.

We have to fight back in the best way we know how -- electing pro-choice Democratic women who will always protect reproductive freedom.

**Donate $3 to help elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight any attempts to block patients' rights to abortion care:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/No-Domestic-Gag-Rule](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5b5f/24c69fd9/1bd029493/188ecf2e/2622747595/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180518_email_DomesticGag?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgNoDomesticGagRul&utm_campaign=WQEFEQ49N_digital_email_20180518_DomesticGag_inact&source=WQEFEQ49N_digital_email_20180518_DomesticGag_inact")**

Thank you,

Christina Reynolds
Vice President of Communications, EMILY's List

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Santa Fe, Texas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
Friends,
As of this writing, at least ten killed and many more injured at a high school in Santa Fe, Texas. We're still learning more, but we know that students went to school this morning and didn't make it to the end of the day. Didn't make it to graduation, didn't make it to the rest of their lives.
My thoughts are with the community of Santa Fe. I'm thinking of the students, teachers, administrators, parents, and law enforcement. I'm deeply sorry that they are experiencing this.
I'm sorry that there are parents, brothers, and sisters who are grieving, who won't see their children and siblings at the next family dinner or baseball game. I'm sorry that there are teachers everywhere worrying about whether their classroom will be next instead of solely focusing on teaching those kids. I'm sorry that some of the students at Santa Fe weren't surprised that this happened because they expected it at some point. I'm sorry that more people have been killed at our schools this year than have been killed while serving in our military.
Either we accept this, and continue to be sorry, and prayerful and thoughtful. Or we act. That's it.
There are 535 members of Congress. Who represent 320 million people. All of us. Each member responsible for representing the interests of this country, of people, of human beings, of kids. Not responsible for PACs or lobbies, corporations or special interests. Not responsible for the next election.
Remembering that, we can meet silence with action. Tragedy with common purpose. The disagreements with compromise and consensus that allow us to do better -- not perfect, not your ideal, not my ideal, but better than what we have today. Shouldn't be too much to ask for the kids who died today, for the kids too scared to go to school on Monday, should it?
What if we required universal background checks to ensure that firearms only get into the hands of those won't harm themselves or someone else. Close all loopholes and exceptions. Every single gun purchase has a background check.
We can stop the new sale of assault weapons and high-capacity magazines to ensure that firearms designed to kill as effectively and efficiently as possible on the battlefield aren't used in our schools, our streets, our churches, and our concerts.
We can give federal help to local school districts to improve on-campus safety. Local stakeholder solutions with assistance to make the necessary investments.
We can fully support federal research on gun violence so that we can better understand and address its root causes.
We can improve record sharing from the background check system, the ability for law enforcement to more effectively act on information that could help in the prevention of gun sales to those who shouldn't own them or stop those who do and pose a threat.
We can find a way to implement Extreme Risk Protective Order (ERPO) or Red Flag Laws. Successfully implementing these can stop shootings and might have a big impact on the suicides that make up 2/3 of our daily 96 gun deaths.
These are some ideas I've heard from my constituents in El Paso, from students at the town hall I held at Montwood High earlier this year, from those I've met along the road throughout the counties of Texas.
These ideas won't prevent every shooting, won't be perfect, aren't easy conversations to have. And there's no way that I would suggest I have all the answers. No one person does.
But any hesitation I have in stepping forward is overwhelmed by the justice demanded by the daily tragedy of gun violence. Any question on how to lead has been answered by gun violence survivors, young people, high school students who are already leading us (forcing us) towards the difficult, important conversation that this country owes them, owes all of us.
I want to use our proud history of gun ownership, gun rights, gun safety, and gun responsibility in Texas as a place from which to lead. I want to sit down with any person of any party in any place and at any time to figure out how we can make sure that this does not continue to happen.
It'll look a lot like our town hall on the steps of the Cottle County courthouse in Paducah on Sunday. I was asked about my stance on gun safety by a woman in the community. She certainly didn't agree with everything I said, and we came to different conclusions about the next steps. But we were able to have the discussion with one another. And we acknowledged that both of us love Texas and want what's best for our country, our schools, our kids.
The open dialogue we've been having across Texas is missing from Congress. The House of Representatives and the Senate owe this country a real discussion, debate and vote on these issues. I will continue to do my best to meet this crisis with the urgency it deserves.
All of Texas grieves today. All of Texas should join in leading the country to act tomorrow.
Sincerely,
Beto

* * *
Paid for by Beto for Texas
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here.](https://secure.betofortexas.com/page/m/6eb92e91/3b1d797/63897ffc/35a86255/63897858/VEsH/ "http://action.betofortexas.com/page/s/fewer-emails") [Click here to unsubscribe.](https://secure.betofortexas.com/page/m/6eb92e91/3b1d797/63897ffc/35a86256/63897858/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://secure.betofortexas.com/page/unsubscribe/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, MAY 18th**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
Dear Derek,
It’s getting busy in New Hampshire!
Last Saturday, the NHRSC held the 2018 Convention at the Inter-Lakes High School in Meredith. The primary purpose of the convention is to discuss potential changes to the Platform. The standing Platform Committee, led by Chairman Chris Ager, did yeoman’s work over a period of several months. So it was disappointing that we didn’t get a chance to consider amendments as delegates voted 2 to 1 to adjourn the meeting with no discussion. The 2016 Platform remains the same.
On Monday, **U.S. Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos** made multiple stops in New Hampshire, including a visit to SNHU and Founders Academy in Manchester. She ended the evening with over 200 Republicans at the Atkinson Resort & Country Club where she gave inspiring remarks on the progress we have made in education.
Thursday night, the **Rockingham County Republican Committee** hosted **Carly Fiorina** with another packed room of Republicans in Atkinson. It was another successful Republican event, with activists and candidates enjoying an evening with Carly.
And tonight, the **NH Log Cabin Republicans** will host the Founders’ Gala at the One Hundred Club in Portsmouth. The keynote speaker is **Guy Benson** , a Fox News contributor and townhall.com political editor.
Republicans continue to get energized as we move into the 2018 election cycle! And the clock is ticking...only 172 days till the November election...
The next three Wednesdays, the NHRSC will host open houses for folks interested in learning more about what it takes to run for office. The open house is from 6 – 7:30 p.m. and anyone who has questions or would like to learn more is welcome to attend. The filing period to run for office begins on June 6 and ends June 15, followed by a Candidates’ Training on Saturday, June 16th (more info to come).
Also in June, the **Belknap County Republican Committee’s annual Lincoln Day Sunset Cruise** on Lake Winnipesaukee and on Thursday the 14th, we will be hosting a celebration of President Trump’s birthday, so watch for details to this free event. clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, MAY 18th | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, It’s getting busy in New Hampshire! Last Saturday, the NHRSC held the 2018 Convention at the Inter-Lakes High School in Meredith. The primary purpose of the convention is to discuss potential changes to the Platform. The standing Platform Committee, led by Chairman Chris Ager, did yeoman’s work over a period of several months. So it was disappointing that we didn’t get a chance to consider amendments as delegates voted 2 to 1 to adjourn the meeting with no discussion. The 2016 Platform remains the same. On Monday, U.S. Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos made multiple stops in New Hampshire, including a visit to SNHU and Founders Academy in Manchester. She ended the evening with over 200 Republicans at the Atkinson Resort & Country Club where she gave inspiring remarks on the progress we have made in education. Thursday night, the Rockingham County Republican Committee hosted Carly Fiorina with another packed room of Republicans in Atkinson. It was another successful Republican event, with activists and candidates enjoying an evening with Carly. And tonight, the NH Log Cabin Republicans will host the Founders’ Gala at the One Hundred Club in Portsmouth. The keynote speaker is Guy Benson , a Fox News contributor and townhall.com political editor. Republicans continue to get energized as we move into the 2018 election cycle! And the clock is ticking...only 172 days till the November election... The next three Wednesdays, the NHRSC will host open houses for folks interested in learning more about what it takes to run for office. The open house is from 6 – 7:30 p.m. and anyone who has questions or would like to learn more is welcome to attend. The filing period to run for office begins on June 6 and ends June 15, followed by a Candidates’ Training on Saturday, June 16th (more info to come). Also in June, the Belknap County Republican Committee’s annual Lincoln Day Sunset Cruise on Lake Winnipesaukee and on Thursday the 14th, we will be hosting a celebration of President Trump’s birthday, so watch for details to this free event. For other upcoming events, visit . NH Republicans have accomplished a lot in this past year---many successes because of Republican leadership in the House, Senate, and corner office. Let’s continue to work together to move our economy forward, let’s make sure we’re all working together to send Republicans back to Concord, and send two Republicans to Congress to help President Trump continue his agenda to Make America Great Again. Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? ** Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. PRIORITIES TAX REFORM SPECIFICS EVENT SPOTLIGHTS | UPCOMING EVENTS PHOTO OF THE WEEK Photo from our Spring to Victory Dinner with Betsy DeVos! REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT Our Republican Spotlight this week shines on Rockingham County GOP Chair Brian Murphy and Freedom Founders Dinner event organizer Diane Bitter! Brian, Diane, and the Rockingham County GOP leadership team put on a great event this past week. Congratulations on the successful event with Carly Fiorina! Diane and Brian are pictured here with RNC Chairwoman Ronna McDaniel when she visited New Hampshire in October 2017. TWEETS**OF THE WEEK Tweet With Us! | | VIDEO OF SENATE PRESIDENT MORSE AT RED TOWN HALL RNC LAUNCHES "PAYCHECK PRESIDENT" WEBSITE House Speaker Gene Chandler Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook Senate President Chuck Morse Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook Executive Council Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook Governor Chris Sununu Web - "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - Web - Facebook - Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: tel:6032259341 "tel:6032259341" | Email: mailto:info@nhgop.org "info@nhgop.org" • • mailto:info@nhgop.org "info@nhgop.org" | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire 10 Water Street Concord New Hampshire 03301
