| Vermonters elected Governor Scott to confront Vermont’s affordability crisis and do what he could to help them keep more of what they earn and make state government more efficient. **Vermonters wanted balance, and a change from the tax-and-spend policies of the past.** For two years, the Governor has been clear with the Legislature: **Vermonters need a break, and _he will not support raising taxes or fees this session! _**

Yet, the legislative leadership passed a budget and tax bill that raises over $33 million in property taxes, even though we have $160 million more in revenue this year without raising taxes.

**The idea that we should raise property taxes by $33 million – especially when we have $160 million over last year – makes no sense to the Governor or most Vermonters.** Since the Legislative leadership declined to work with him to address his concerns during the regular session, the Governor will veto these bills. And, **the Governor must exercise his constitutional authority to call a special session of the Legislature in order to prevent an unnecessary, unaffordable, and unacceptable tax hike on Vermonters. **

The Governor has asked the Speaker of the House and Senate Pro Tem to begin a series of meetings prior to the Special Session. Ideally, consensus could be reached before Wednesday to allow for a budget and education funding bill to be passed quickly.

At this point, the Legislature has two options:
1. Work together with the Administration to prevent this unnecessary property tax rate increase, (there are several paths forward); or
2. Pass tax increases again, and attempt to override the veto so their unnecessary tax hikes would become law.

In case there is any ambiguity about Governor Scott’s position, we will reiterate it here: **If the Legislature wants to raise taxes this year, it will have to override a veto of the budget and tax bill. **

How long it will take the Legislature to reach one of these two decisions points is entirely up to the Speaker and the Senate President Pro Tem. But there is absolutely no reason an agreement, or override, can’t happen long before the July 1 deadline approaches. But, again, that timing and the decision of which path to take -- an unnecessary tax increase or property tax relief -- is up to the legislative leadership.

**We hope they do the right thing and join Governor Scott in focusing on growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable. **

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | Vermonters elected Governor Scott to confront Vermont’s affordability crisis and do what he could to help them keep more of what they earn and make state government more efficient. Vermonters wanted balance, and a change from the tax-and-spend policies of the past. For two years, the Governor has been clear with the Legislature: Vermonters need a break, and he will not support raising taxes or fees this session! Yet, the legislative leadership passed a budget and tax bill that raises over $33 million in property taxes, even though we have $160 million more in revenue this year without raising taxes. The idea that we should raise property taxes by $33 million – especially when we have $160 million over last year – makes no sense to the Governor or most Vermonters. Since the Legislative leadership declined to work with him to address his concerns during the regular session, the Governor will veto these bills. And, the Governor must exercise his constitutional authority to call a special session of the Legislature in order to prevent an unnecessary, unaffordable, and unacceptable tax hike on Vermonters. The Governor has asked the Speaker of the House and Senate Pro Tem to begin a series of meetings prior to the Special Session. Ideally, consensus could be reached before Wednesday to allow for a budget and education funding bill to be passed quickly. At this point, the Legislature has two options: 1. Work together with the Administration to prevent this unnecessary property tax rate increase, (there are several paths forward); or 2. Pass tax increases again, and attempt to override the veto so their unnecessary tax hikes would become law. In case there is any ambiguity about Governor Scott’s position, we will reiterate it here: If the Legislature wants to raise taxes this year, it will have to override a veto of the budget and tax bill. How long it will take the Legislature to reach one of these two decisions points is entirely up to the Speaker and the Senate President Pro Tem. But there is absolutely no reason an agreement, or override, can’t happen long before the July 1 deadline approaches. But, again, that timing and the decision of which path to take -- an unnecessary tax increase or property tax relief -- is up to the legislative leadership. We hope they do the right thing and join Governor Scott in focusing on growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable. | "DONATE TODAY!" | | | | | | | | | | | | Phil Scott for Vermont, Glen Wright, Treasurer, P.O. Box 988, Montpelier, VT, 05602 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or

Peter,

I believe in order to serve the people of Montana, I need to understand what issues you find most important.

[Please take my quick survey](http://troydowningforussenate.cmail19.com/t/d-l-aihhir-yhdyitiujj-n/) and let me know what your priorities for Montana are.

This campaign is about draining the swamp in Washington, so that your voice can be clearly heard, and I want to make sure I’m doing as good of a job as I can representing you.

[Click here](http://troydowningforussenate.cmail19.com/t/d-l-aihhir-yhdyitiujj-p/) to let me know what matters to you most.

Together, we can bring change to Montana and to the American government.

Called to Serve,
Troy

P.S. It’s time that we got rid of the career politicians and special interests that are ignoring average Americans and destroying the American Dream. My call to serve will be for you. name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: One Team
body:
It's officially general election season, and we kicked it off with some BIG news this week. You've all probably heard the media hyperventilating... | | |
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4a89997f9f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_One Team_** It's officially general election season, and we kicked it off with some BIG news this week. You've all probably heard the media hyperventilating over our "contentious" primary, and whether Republican would "come together." Well, we now have proof that the media got this one wrong.

That's because we got [polling back yesterday](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3426b49766&e=1acd9bb3d6) that shows that Hoosier Republicans are already unifying as ONE TEAM...ready to retire Democrat Joe Donnelly, and replace him in the U.S. Senate with Mike Braun, a conservative outsider who's ready to take on business as usual in Washington.

The poll shows that just one week after the primary, Mike Braun is already leading Democrat Donnelly by one point. And with Hoosier Republicans unified around supporting Braun and helping share his message, our numbers will continue to climb. (You can help right now by [sharing this post](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7391f6c761&e=1acd9bb3d6) on Facebook about this latest polling.)

We're one team...and we'll be one team again at two big events coming up soon. The first is Monday's big [Indiana Republican Party Spring Dinner](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e6b0049a43&e=1acd9bb3d6) in Indianapolis, when we'll hear from two Hoosiers who are leaders behind-the-scenes in the Trump-Pence administration. They'll be telling the fascinating story of 2016, and providing an inside look into the 2016 campaign and how President Trump is advancing change in Washington.

And then this team will all be together again in just three weeks for the [2018 Indiana Republican State Convention](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=72b9fd6f83&e=1acd9bb3d6) in Evansville, when we'll be formally nominating our historic all-female statewide ticket and rallying the troops for big victories this fall. It's going to be a blast!

We're unifed. We're ONE TEAM. And we're ready to go.

-Kyle _Pictured: Chairman Kyle Hupfer and Senator Todd Young join our 2018 Indiana Republican Party summer intern class on their first day today! Thanks to Senator Young, we'll have a team of 33 interns stationed across the state, talking to voters and sharing our winning message! If you see one of them in your community, make sure to say hello! _
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| **_Waving Indiana's Green Flag_**
It's May, and you know what that means! It's officially racing season in Indiana, and [Governor Eric Holcomb was there front-and-center](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=879ea43d6e&e=1acd9bb3d6) at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway, waving the green flag on a productive day of practices.

And just like the drivers, Indiana's on track for a big month! That's because Governor Eric Holcomb is going not on one, but TWO, economic development trade missons this month. Last week, he was in Israel, meeting with government leaders (like Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu) and business leaders in the agriculture and technology industries. And today, he's leaving for Europe...where he'll be meeting with businesses from six countries in seven days. He's then returning to Indiana on the first EVER direct flight from Europe to Indianapolis.

Check out [video of Governor Holcomb waving the green flag](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0b6cc99dc8&e=1acd9bb3d6) this week...and [follow his Facebook page](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f215be48e9&e=1acd9bb3d6) to keep up-to-date on his work telling and selling Indiana's story around the world!
---
|
---
| | _**The Mike Braun Corner**_
---
|
---
| **_Braun Takes the Lead!_**
In case you missed it, conservative outsider Mike Braun got big news yesterday! Just one week after clinching the Republican nomination for U.S. Senate, he's already taken the lead in the race, with a one-point lead over Democrat Joe Donnelly! **[Share this news on Facebook!](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=82c1bf85b8&e=1acd9bb3d6)**
---
|
---
|
---
| _**Last Chance: Attend the 2018 Spring Party Dinner!**_
Last chance! Monday, Hoosier Republicans will come together for the 2018 Indiana GOP Spring Dinner.

We're bringing in two Hoosiers -- who have gone on to become key players within the Trump-Pence administration -- to provide an inside look into the White House, the 2016 campaign, how Gov. Pence became Vice President Pence, and how they helped deliver national victory on that legendary night in November.

You're going to want to be there this Monday, so [reserve your seats NOW](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c53cb0e959&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
|
---
|
---
| **_Three Weeks: Indiana GOP State Convention _**
Today officially marks the beginning of the three-week countdown until the [2018 Indiana Republican State Convention in Evansville](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=00abb876ac&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

The deadline to register as a delegate has now passed (please make sure you've [paid your delegate fees](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9f6c803aef&e=1acd9bb3d6)), but there's still time to [register as a convention guest](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9998e403aa&e=1acd9bb3d6). As a guest, you'll have exclusive access to our **HUGE** Friday night party in downtown Evansville (with great food and a performance by The Hunter Smith Band!), and will get to hear from our packed line-up of all-star speakers on Saturday (including RNC Chairwoman Ronna McDaniel!).

You won't want to miss the convention, so [reserve your spot today](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c94a9094f8&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
|
---
| _**Upcoming Republican Events**_
[May 21](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bf50cc34a5&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- 2018 Indiana Republican Spring Dinner
[June 8-9](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3cae5ce61a&e=1acd9bb3d6): 2018 Indiana Republican State Convention in Evansville
---
|
---
| _**News You Can Use**_
---
|
---
Vice President Mike Pence is in Indiana today, headlining a rally to support the Trump-Pence administration's federal tax cuts, which are already sparking our economy and putting more money in Hoosier pockets!

Follow Vice President Pence's visit today [on Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bc8045ea4c&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
This week, Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer [published an op-ed](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=73847a57d9&e=1acd9bb3d6) setting the stage for Indiana's big U.S. Senate race, with conservative outsider Mike Braun taking on Democrat Joe Donnelly.

It's already being called the number one race in the country! [Check it out](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b4e56457dc&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
|
---
Indiana Attorney General Curtis Hill is working alongside Governor Eric Holcomb to combat Indiana's drug epidemic!

This week, he announced a new partnership with 49 CVS Pharmacy locations throughout the state to launch new [drug-disposal sites for old prescriptions](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2d1b51c9cf&e=1acd9bb3d6). There's also a [new grant coming to Indiana](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=af6e2125a6&e=1acd9bb3d6), targeting drug dealers who are funneling drugs through the stage

Great work, AG Hill and Governor Holcomb!
---
Great work, Secretary of State Connie Lawson, for helping streamline the way businesses interact with their state government!

Indiana's one-stop business portal, INBiz, [now has 300,000 active users](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=86e9c1ec86&e=1acd9bb3d6)! Nice job providing great government service!
|
---
---
---
